title: Chapter 10: Noun Declensions
markdown:
# Chapter 10: Noun Declensions

## Strong Declension

### Introduction
Strong declension (starke Deklination) is the most common noun declension pattern. It follows the same endings as masculine definite articles (der/die/das).

### Masculine Nouns (der-words)

Most masculine nouns are strong declension. They take the same endings as "der".

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | der Mann | die Männer |
| **Accusative** | den Mann | die Männer |
| **Dative** | dem Mann | den Männern |
| **Genitive** | des Mannes | der Männer |

**Examples:**
- **der Hund** (dog): des Hundes, dem Hund, den Hund
- **der Garten** (garden): des Gartens, dem Garten, den Garten
- **der Apfel** (apple): des Apfels, dem Apfel, den Apfel

**Note:** Plural forms are regular - add "-(e)n" or "-e" depending on the noun.

### Neuter Nouns (das-words)

Most neuter nouns are strong declension. They take the same endings as "das".

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | das Haus | die Häuser |
| **Accusative** | das Haus | die Häuser |
| **Dative** | dem Haus | den Häusern |
| **Genitive** | des Hauses | der Häuser |

**Examples:**
- **das Kind** (child): des Kindes, dem Kind, den Kindern
- **das Buch** (book): des Buches, dem Buch, den Büchern
- **das Auto** (car): des Autos, dem Auto, den Autos

### Feminine Nouns (die-words)

Feminine nouns have simplified strong declension - they are the same in nominative and accusative.

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | die Frau | die Frauen |
| **Accusative** | die Frau | die Frauen |
| **Dative** | der Frau | den Frauen |
| **Genitive** | der Frau | der Frauen |

**Examples:**
- **die Schule** (school): der Schule, den Schulen
- **die Tür** (door): der Tür, den Türen
- **die Uhr** (clock): der Uhr, den Uhren

**Note:** Feminine nouns don't get an extra ending in dative singular - they stay the same as genitive.

### Strong Declension Rules

1. **Masculine nouns**: Usually strong (der Hund, der Mann)
2. **Neuter nouns**: Usually strong (das Haus, das Buch)
3. **Feminine nouns**: Always strong (die Frau, die Schule)
4. **Plural**: All genders take "-n"/"-en" in dative plural

**Exceptions:**
- Some masculine nouns are weak (see weak declension)
- Diminutives in -chen/-lein are always neuter: das Mädchen, das Büchlein

---

## Weak Declension

### Introduction
Weak declension (schwache Deklination) applies to certain masculine nouns. They take "-en" or "-n" endings in all cases except nominative singular.

### Masculine Weak Nouns

Weak nouns are mostly masculine and refer to:
- **People**: der Student, der Tourist, der Polizist
- **Professions**: der Lehrer, der Arzt, der Koch
- **Animals**: der Elefant, der Bär (bear)
- **Some others**: der Planet, der Diamant

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | der Student | die Studenten |
| **Accusative** | den Studenten | die Studenten |
| **Dative** | dem Studenten | den Studenten |
| **Genitive** | des Studenten | der Studenten |

**Examples:**
- **der Junge** (boy): des Jungen, dem Jungen, den Jungen
- **der Herr** (gentleman): des Herrn, dem Herrn, den Herrn
- **der Mensch** (human): des Menschen, dem Menschen, den Menschen

### Key Characteristics

1. **All cases except nominative singular** get "-en" ending
2. **Plural** is regular with "-en"
3. **Genitive singular** gets "-en" (not "-es" like strong)

### Common Weak Nouns

**People:**
- der Junge, der Knabe, der Bub
- der Herr, der Mann (in some contexts)
- der Student, der Schüler, der Lehrer
- der Arzt, der Patient, der Tourist

**Professions:**
- der Koch, der Bäcker, der Friseur
- der Polizist, der Soldat, der Pilot

**Animals:**
- der Elefant, der Bär, der Löwe
- der Affe, der Hase

### Special Cases

**der Herr** (Mr./gentleman):
- Nominative: der Herr
- All other cases: dem Herrn, den Herrn, des Herrn

**der Name** (name) - mixed declension (see below)

---

## Mixed Declension

### Introduction
Mixed declension (gemischte Deklination) combines features of strong and weak declension. It applies to certain masculine and neuter nouns.

### Masculine Mixed Nouns

These nouns have strong endings in nominative/accusative singular, but weak endings elsewhere.

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | der See | die Seen |
| **Accusative** | den See | die Seen |
| **Dative** | dem See | den Seen |
| **Genitive** | des Sees | der Seen |

**Common mixed masculine nouns:**
- der See (lake)
- der Schmerz (pain)
- der Staat (state)
- der Strahl (ray/beam)
- der Teil (part)

### Neuter Mixed Nouns

Most important: **das Herz** (heart) and **das Ohr** (ear)

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | das Herz | die Herzen |
| **Accusative** | das Herz | die Herzen |
| **Dative** | dem Herzen | den Herzen |
| **Genitive** | des Herzens | der Herzen |

**Examples:**
- **das Herz**: des Herzens, dem Herzen, den Herzen
- **das Ohr**: des Ohres, dem Ohr, den Ohren

### Special Cases

**der Name** (name) - irregular mixed:
- Nominative: der Name
- Accusative: den Namen
- Dative: dem Namen
- Genitive: des Namens
- Plural: die Namen

**der Gedanke** (thought) - irregular mixed:
- Nominative: der Gedanke
- Accusative: den Gedanken
- Dative: dem Gedanken
- Genitive: des Gedankens

---

## Practice Exercises

### Exercise 1: Strong Declension
Decline the following nouns in all cases (singular and plural):

1. der Mann (man)
   - Nom. sg.: __________
   - Acc. sg.: __________
   - Dat. sg.: __________
   - Gen. sg.: __________
   - Nom. pl.: __________
   - Acc. pl.: __________
   - Dat. pl.: __________
   - Gen. pl.: __________

2. das Buch (book)
   - Nom. sg.: __________
   - Acc. sg.: __________
   - Dat. sg.: __________
   - Gen. sg.: __________
   - Nom. pl.: __________
   - Acc. pl.: __________
   - Dat. pl.: __________
   - Gen. pl.: __________

3. die Frau (woman)
   - Nom. sg.: __________
   - Acc. sg.: __________
   - Dat. sg.: __________
   - Gen. sg.: __________
   - Nom. pl.: __________
   - Acc. pl.: __________
   - Dat. pl.: __________
   - Gen. pl.: __________

### Exercise 2: Weak Declension
Complete the declension table for "der Student":

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| Nominative | der Student | __________ |
| Accusative | __________ | __________ |
| Dative | __________ | __________ |
| Genitive | __________ | __________ |

### Exercise 3: Mixed Declension
Decline "das Herz" in all cases:

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| Nominative | das Herz | __________ |
| Accusative | __________ | __________ |
| Dative | __________ | __________ |
| Genitive | __________ | __________ |

### Exercise 4: Identify Declension Type
Classify the following nouns as strong, weak, or mixed:

1. der Lehrer ___ (teacher)
2. das Kind ___ (child)
3. der See ___ (lake)
4. die Schule ___ (school)
5. der Herr ___ (gentleman)
6. das Herz ___ (heart)
7. der Student ___ (student)
8. die Tür ___ (door)

### Exercise 5: Complete Sentences
Fill in the correct noun forms:

1. Ich gebe ___ (der Mann) das Buch.
2. Das Auto gehört ___ (der Vater).
3. Sie wohnt bei ___ (die Eltern).
4. Das ist das Haus ___ (der Nachbar).
5. Er hilft ___ (der Student).
6. Das Buch liegt auf ___ (der Tisch).

### Exercise 6: Genitive Constructions
Complete with correct genitive forms:

1. Das ist das Auto ___ (mein Bruder).
2. Die Farbe ___ (die Wand) ist weiß.
3. Am Ende ___ (die Straße) steht ein Baum.
4. Während ___ (die Ferien) reisen wir.
5. Trotz ___ (das Wetter) gehen wir spazieren.

---

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

1. **Wrong plural dative endings**
   - ❌ die Männer (nominative plural used as dative)
   - ✅ den Männern

2. **Feminine dative confusion**
   - ❌ der Frauen (trying to add ending)
   - ✅ den Frauen

3. **Weak noun genitive**
   - ❌ des Student (strong ending)
   - ✅ des Studenten (weak ending)

4. **Mixed declension dative**
   - ❌ dem See (strong ending)
   - ✅ dem Seen (mixed: -en)

5. **Gender mistakes**
   - ❌ der Mädchen (masculine article)
   - ✅ das Mädchen (neuter diminutive)

6. **Irregular nouns**
   - ❌ des Name (missing -ns)
   - ✅ des Namens

---

## Summary

- **Strong declension**: Most common
  - Masculine: der Hund → des Hundes, dem Hund, den Hund
  - Neuter: das Buch → des Buches, dem Buch, den Büchern
  - Feminine: die Frau → der Frau, den Frauen

- **Weak declension**: Certain masculine nouns (-en in all cases except nom. sg.)
  - der Student → des Studenten, dem Studenten, den Studenten
  - People and professions: Lehrer, Arzt, Polizist

- **Mixed declension**: Combination of strong and weak
  - der See → des Sees, dem See, den Seen
  - das Herz → des Herzens, dem Herzen, den Herzen

- **Rules of thumb**:
  - Feminine nouns: always strong, simplified endings
  - Most masculine/neuter: strong declension
  - People/professions: often weak
  - Learn irregular ones individually

---

## Vocabulary List

- **die Deklination** - declension
- **stark** - strong
- **schwach** - weak
- **gemischt** - mixed
- **der Kasus** / **die Kasus** - case
- **das Genus** / **die Genera** - gender
- **der Numerus** / **die Numeri** - number
- **maskulin** - masculine
- **feminin** - feminine
- **neutral** - neuter
- **der Singular** - singular
- **der Plural** - plural
- **die Endung** / **die Endungen** - ending
- **regelmäßig** - regular
- **unregelmäßig** - irregular
- **das Substantiv** / **die Substantive** - noun
- **der Mensch** / **die Menschen** - human/person
- **das Tier** / **die Tiere** - animal
- **der Beruf** / **die Berufe** - profession

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Strong declension (starke Deklination) is the most common noun declension pattern. It follows the same endings as masculine definite articles (der/die/das).

sections.title: Masculine Nouns (der-words)
sections.content:
Most masculine nouns are strong declension. They take the same endings as "der".

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | der Mann | die Männer |
| **Accusative** | den Mann | die Männer |
| **Dative** | dem Mann | den Männern |
| **Genitive** | des Mannes | der Männer |

**Examples:**
- **der Hund** (dog): des Hundes, dem Hund, den Hund
- **der Garten** (garden): des Gartens, dem Garten, den Garten
- **der Apfel** (apple): des Apfels, dem Apfel, den Apfel

**Note:** Plural forms are regular - add "-(e)n" or "-e" depending on the noun.

sections.title: Neuter Nouns (das-words)
sections.content:
Most neuter nouns are strong declension. They take the same endings as "das".

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | das Haus | die Häuser |
| **Accusative** | das Haus | die Häuser |
| **Dative** | dem Haus | den Häusern |
| **Genitive** | des Hauses | der Häuser |

**Examples:**
- **das Kind** (child): des Kindes, dem Kind, den Kindern
- **das Buch** (book): des Buches, dem Buch, den Büchern
- **das Auto** (car): des Autos, dem Auto, den Autos

sections.title: Feminine Nouns (die-words)
sections.content:
Feminine nouns have simplified strong declension - they are the same in nominative and accusative.

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | die Frau | die Frauen |
| **Accusative** | die Frau | die Frauen |
| **Dative** | der Frau | den Frauen |
| **Genitive** | der Frau | der Frauen |

**Examples:**
- **die Schule** (school): der Schule, den Schulen
- **die Tür** (door): der Tür, den Türen
- **die Uhr** (clock): der Uhr, den Uhren

**Note:** Feminine nouns don't get an extra ending in dative singular - they stay the same as genitive.

sections.title: Strong Declension Rules
sections.content:
1. **Masculine nouns**: Usually strong (der Hund, der Mann)
2. **Neuter nouns**: Usually strong (das Haus, das Buch)
3. **Feminine nouns**: Always strong (die Frau, die Schule)
4. **Plural**: All genders take "-n"/"-en" in dative plural

**Exceptions:**
- Some masculine nouns are weak (see weak declension)
- Diminutives in -chen/-lein are always neuter: das Mädchen, das Büchlein

---

Weak nouns are mostly masculine and refer to:
- **People**: der Student, der Tourist, der Polizist
- **Professions**: der Lehrer, der Arzt, der Koch

- **Some others**: der Planet, der Diamant

| **Accusative** | den Studenten | die Studenten |
| **Dative** | dem Studenten | den Studenten |
| **Genitive** | des Studenten | der Studenten |

**Examples:**
- **der Junge** (boy): des Jungen, dem Jungen, den Jungen
- **der Herr** (gentleman): des Herrn, dem Herrn, den Herrn
- **der Mensch** (human): des Menschen, dem Menschen, den Menschen

2. **Plural** is regular with "-en"
3. **Genitive singular** gets "-en" (not "-es" like strong)

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Mixed declension (gemischte Deklination) combines features of strong and weak declension. It applies to certain masculine and neuter nouns.

sections.title: Masculine Mixed Nouns

These nouns have strong endings in nominative/accusative singular, but weak endings elsewhere.

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | der See | die Seen |
| **Accusative** | den See | die Seen |
| **Dative** | dem See | den Seen |
| **Genitive** | des Sees | der Seen |

**Common mixed masculine nouns:**
- der See (lake)
- der Schmerz (pain)
- der Staat (state)
- der Strahl (ray/beam)

sections.content:
Most important: **das Herz** (heart) and **das Ohr** (ear)

| Case | Singular | Plural |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Nominative** | das Herz | die Herzen |
| **Accusative** | das Herz | die Herzen |
| **Dative** | dem Herzen | den Herzen |
| **Genitive** | des Herzens | der Herzen |

**Examples:**
- **das Herz**: des Herzens, dem Herzen, den Herzen
- **das Ohr**: des Ohres, dem Ohr, den Ohren

sections.content:
**der Name** (name) - irregular mixed:
- Nominative: der Name
- Accusative: den Namen
- Dative: dem Namen
- Genitive: des Namens
- Plural: die Namen

**der Gedanke** (thought) - irregular mixed:
- Nominative: der Gedanke
- Accusative: den Gedanken
- Dative: dem Gedanken
- Genitive: des Gedankens

sections.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
sections.content:
1. **Wrong plural dative endings**
   - ❌ die Männer (nominative plural used as dative)
   - ✅ den Männern

2. **Feminine dative confusion**
   - ❌ der Frauen (trying to add ending)
   - ✅ den Frauen

3. **Weak noun genitive**
   - ❌ des Student (strong ending)
   - ✅ des Studenten (weak ending)

4. **Mixed declension dative**
   - ❌ dem See (strong ending)
   - ✅ dem Seen (mixed: -en)

5. **Gender mistakes**
   - ❌ der Mädchen (masculine article)
   - ✅ das Mädchen (neuter diminutive)

6. **Irregular nouns**
   - ❌ des Name (missing -ns)
   - ✅ des Namens

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
- **Strong declension**: Most common
  - Masculine: der Hund → des Hundes, dem Hund, den Hund
  - Neuter: das Buch → des Buches, dem Buch, den Büchern
  - Feminine: die Frau → der Frau, den Frauen

- **Weak declension**: Certain masculine nouns (-en in all cases except nom. sg.)
  - der Student → des Studenten, dem Studenten, den Studenten
  - People and professions: Lehrer, Arzt, Polizist

- **Mixed declension**: Combination of strong and weak
  - der See → des Sees, dem See, den Seen
  - das Herz → des Herzens, dem Herzen, den Herzen

- **Rules of thumb**:
  - Feminine nouns: always strong, simplified endings
  - Most masculine/neuter: strong declension
  - People/professions: often weak
  - Learn irregular ones individually

---
